meta.name: Misir Wat (Ethiopian Red Lentil Stew)
meta.subtitle: Berbere-forward lentil wot with long-cooked onions and niter kibbeh (or oil).

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Canonical misir wat pattern: onion reduction + berbere bloom + lentil simmer until creamy.
Designed to be vegan-capable by default (fat choice), while preserving Ethiopian flavor profile.
SSA-friendly signals included for meal planning and batch cooking.

summary: A deeply savory Ethiopian lentil stew built on slow-cooked onions and berbere, simmered until creamy and traditionally served with injera.

yield.notes: Serves ~6 with injera/rice; ~8 as part of a multi-dish platter.

optional.label: Immersion blender
optional.notes: Optional: blend a small portion for extra creaminess.

notes: Use niter kibbeh/ghee for traditional richness (contains dairy).

ingredients.label: Red lentils, rinsed
ingredients.notes: Rinse until water runs mostly clear to reduce foam.

ingredients.label: Yellow onions, very finely minced
notes: Misir wat uses a generous onion base; mince fine for a smooth stew.
Food processor is okay if you pulse to mince (not puree).

ingredients.label: Niter kibbeh (spiced clarified butter) OR neutral oil
Berbere spice blend
notes: Typical range: 12–24 g depending on blend heat.
Add in 2 additions to bloom without scorching.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Optional but common; boosts color and savory depth.

ingredients.label: Paprika (sweet)
ingredients.notes: Optional: adds color if your berbere is mild.

ingredients.label: Water or vegetable stock
ingredients.notes: Use water for a clean berbere-forward profile; stock for richer body.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice (optional, to brighten)
ingredients.notes: A small splash at the end can lift flavors.

notes: Texture control: for thicker stew, simmer uncovered for the last 8–12 minutes.
If lentils thicken too much, thin with hot water a little at a time.
If foam rises early, skim lightly; rinsing lentils helps reduce foam.

changes.technique: Blend 1–2 cups of cooked stew with an immersion blender, then stir back in.

changes.berbere: Increase berbere to 24 g, or add a pinch of cayenne if your berbere is mild.

changes.fat: Use neutral oil instead of niter kibbeh/ghee; verify stock is vegetable-only.

notes: Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat to a simmer before serving.

freezer.notes: Freeze in portions; thaw overnight and reheat gently with a splash of water.

leftoversPlan: Day 2: Serve with greens and rice.
Day 3: Thin slightly and use as a sauce over roasted vegetables.

steps.title: Rinse lentils
instructions: Rinse red lentils in a fine mesh strainer until the water runs mostly clear.

steps.title: Begin onion reduction (dry, then fat)
instructions: Add minced onions to a heavy pot over medium heat.
Cook, stirring often, until onions release moisture and begin to soften and reduce.
If onions start to stick, add 2–3 tbsp water and scrape the bottom; continue reducing.
checks: Onions are softened and reduced; no browning.

steps.title: Add fat and cook onions until jammy
instructions: Cook on medium-low, stirring frequently, until onions are jammy and aromatic and moisture has mostly cooked off.
checks: Onions are thick and glossy; raw onion smell is gone.

steps.title: Bloom aromatics and berbere
instructions: Stir in garlic and ginger; cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
Add berbere in 2 additions, stirring constantly to bloom.
If using tomato paste, add it now and cook 1–2 minutes to deepen flavor and color.
checks: Spices are fragrant and not scorched.

steps.title: Add water and lentils, then simmer
instructions: Add water (or vegetable stock) and stir to dissolve the onion-spice base into the liquid.
Add rinsed lentils and bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer partially covered, stirring occasionally, until lentils break down and stew becomes creamy.
checks: Lentils are very soft and the stew looks creamy.

steps.title: Adjust texture and season
instructions: Stir in salt and simmer 3–6 minutes so seasoning integrates.
If stew is too thick, add hot water a splash at a time; if too thin, simmer uncovered to reduce.
Optional: add a small splash of lemon juice to brighten.
checks: Stew is seasoned, creamy, and spoonable.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot with injera (traditional) or rice (fallback).
For platter-style meals, pair with greens (gomen) and vegetable stews.
checks: Stew is hot and ready to serve.

change.notes: Use neutral oil; keep bloom step the same.
Use 10–12 g berbere and add paprika for color.
Simmer uncovered to thicken; add hot water to thin.
